name: Kawaiaha'o Church
tagline: The 'Westminster Abbey of Hawaii' — built from 14,000 coral blocks in 1842, where Hawaiian royalty worshipped and history was made.
parking: Street parking on Punchbowl St; paid lots nearby
hours: Open for self-guided visits Mon-Fri 8am-4pm; Sunday services at 9am (Hawaiian language)
bestTime: Sunday 9am service for the Hawaiian-language hymns — profoundly moving even if you don't understand the words
talkStory: Completed in 1842, its 14,000 coral blocks were cut by hand from offshore reefs by Hawaiian divers working at depths of 20 feet — construction took five years. King Kamehameha III spoke 'Ua mau ke ea o ka aina i ka pono' here in 1843 after British sovereignty was reversed — now Hawaii's state motto. The rear cemetery holds missionaries, royalty, and sailors from the Thaddeus. The Sunday 9am Hawaiian-language service features hymns that will give you chicken skin.
